checks.a_analysis: The output document represents each piece of information from the source PDF exactly once. There is no evidence of paragraphs, section headers, or agenda items being repeated.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard structure. The document title 'Federal Interagency Committee' is assigned as H1 (reading order 1), and the 'Agenda:' section title is assigned as H2 (reading order 4). There are no skipped levels or incorrectly nested headers.
The source document does not contain any tables, and the output correctly reflects this by not generating any table elements.

The output preserves the reading order of the source document exactly: Title, followed by metadata (Date/Location), followed by Remote Access information, followed by the Agenda section header and the agenda items.

The agenda in the source is a chronological list of times and topics. In the output (element 5), this is represented as a single paragraph block with double-newlines separating the items. While not converted into a formal list structure, the meaning and structural separation of the items are maintained accurately through line breaks, which is an acceptable conversion choice for this format.
The WebEx URL present in the source is captured accurately in the output text. No external links have been hallucinated, and the source URL is faithfully reproduced.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF presents the 'Agenda' as a vertical list of timed events, which is standard for an agenda document to ensure readability and quick reference. The HTML output collapses this list into a single, continuous paragraph block. This significantly alters the visual organization and flow intended by the source document, making the schedule much harder to scan. Additionally, the 'Remote access' and 'WebEx' information sections, which were vertically structured in the PDF, have also been collapsed into single text blocks in the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'Agenda' section shows a vertical list of items starting with times (e.g., '10:00 Welcome...', '10:05 Response...', etc.) each on its own line.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Agenda' section is rendered as a single paragraph of text: '10:00 Welcome (Scott Lokken, FIC Chair) 10:05 Response to Hurricane Florence...'
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda section, bottom half of page 1

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF does not contain any tables. Therefore, there are no tables to misrender in the HTML output.
The source PDF is a text-only document and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or charts.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While 'Federal Interagency Committee' appears twice at the top, the first instance is a page/document title used for navigation/identification and the second is the document header, which is acceptable behavior according to the instructions.
